associativeOnly: Report PTM site enrichment as statistical associations only
Treat protein-disease overlays as suggestive, never diagnostic
PTM stoichiometry is not localization confidence — keep them distinct in narrative
requiresEvidence: PTM site claims must cite localization probability (e.g. PhosphoRS, ptmRS, A-score) AND search-engine FDR
Protein quantification claims must cite the quantification method (LFQ, TMT, iBAQ) and the quantitation FDR
Pathway-enrichment claims on proteins must cite the pathway database version + the enrichment method (e.g. fgsea, gprofiler)

evidenceRules.description: PTM site assignments must cite a localization-probability column (PhosphoRS, ptmRS, A-score, ascore_localization). Sites without a localization confidence are flagged as ambiguous and downgraded from positional to peptide-level claims.
Protein abundance claims must cite the quantification method (LFQ / TMT / iBAQ / SILAC) AND the quantitation FDR. A bare 'protein X was elevated' without method+FDR is treated as associative-only.

Narratives must keep PTM-site stoichiometry (the fraction of a peptide that is modified) distinct from PTM-site localization confidence. Confusing the two is a reproducibility hazard the verifier surfaces as a ClaimVerificationReport mismatch.

note: Proteomics overlay (S3.16). UniProt accession patterns ([OPQA][0-9][A-Z0-9]{3}[0-9] for canonical 6-char IDs, [A-NR-Z][0-9]([A-Z][A-Z0-9]{2}[0-9]){1,2} for 10-char IDs). PRIDE/MASSIVE accession patterns (PXD/PRD/MSV). PTM-aware direction vocab (hyperphosphorylated etc.). LFQ/TMT/iBAQ effect-size columns plus standard logFC. evidenceRules require localization probability for PTM sites and quantification method for abundance claims. Merged on top of interpretation-policy.json at load time.
